title: lippia bicolor
intro: Lippia bicolor, also known as Mexican oregano or Mexican thyme, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Verbenaceae family and is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lippia bicolor include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in cooking and as a natural insect repellent.
